def: = āeṅtī-pāʼeṅtī, q.v. s.f. The head and foot of a bed; the upper and lower end (of anything), this side and that; — adj. & adv. Lower; beneath.

ety: Zend āba, rt. bā; S. ābhā, rt. bhā
def: s.m. The sun; the musk-deer.
s.m. Serpent; viper; asp; basilisk.
more: (vulg. afīﻌ ) s. m. A serpent, basilisk, asp, viper.

def: s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
s.f. An imitative sound (as of a dog barking), bow-wow.
more: An imitative sound (as, of a dog barking).

def: s.m. pl. (of P. افغان afg̠ān), The people of Afgānistān, the Afgān race.
s.m. pl. (of افق ufq, or ufuq), Horizons; quarters of the heavens; quarters of the world; regions.
more: s. plur. (of افق) Horizons, quarters of the heaven or of the earth, regions.

def: s.m. pl. (of فعل fěʻl), Actions, deeds; conduct.
s.m. pl. (of ʻifrīt), Wicked or malignant genii or spirits, horrible demons or giants.
s.f. Bane, pest, plague; any evil affection; evil, disaster, trouble, misfortune, calamity; wretchedness, misery, hardship, difficulty; (cf. S. āpat)
more: s. f. Calamity, misfortune, wretchedness. (Sans. आपत् ). آفت رسیده āfat-rasīda, or آفت زده āfat-zada, Overtaken or oppressed by calamity; unfortunate, wretched.
subs: (-par), āfat paṛnā (-par), āfat ṭūṭnā (-par), Calamity to befall, trouble or evil to come (upon); disaster, or a disturbance, to arise
v.n. To suffer pain, affliction, trouble, &c.; to bear up against adversity, difficulties, &c.; to raise trouble, difficulties, &c.; to create a disturbance; to inflict trouble
(or āfateṅ) ḍālnā, v.t. To interpose difficulties or troubles, to cause misfortune
āfat-zada, part. adj. & s.m. Overtaken by calamity, involved in misfortune; unfortunate, wretched; — unfortunate creature, poor wretch
s.m. A very wicked person; a sharp, astute fellow
s.f. Bad news, intelligence of a death; a talisman to work mischief
āfat-meṅ phaṅsnā, āfat-meṅ mubtilā honā, v.n. To be involved in misfortune, trouble, &c.; to be overwhelmed with misery, affliction, &c.; to be entangled in difficulties.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آفَت (āfat), borrowed from Arabic آفَة (ʔāfa).

def: s.f. pl. (of آفت āfat), Evils, troubles, misfortunes, calamities, dangers, &c. ( = āfateṅ, see āfat).
more: s. plur. (of آفت) Calamities, misfortunes, evils, dangers.

def: s.m. Lamentation, &c. = فغان fig̠ān, q.v. intj. Alas!
more: 1. s. m. See فغان fig̠ẖān. 2. n. prop. A people, called also Paṭhāns, inhabiting the hilly country to the N. W. of Lāhaur. In their history, they pretend to derive their origin, as well as name, from Afg̠ẖān, whom they account a grandson of Malik Tālūt (king Saul). As to religion, they are Mahometans, notwithstanding the claim they set up to a Jewish extraction. (Plur. Arab. افاغنه afāg̠ẖina or Pers. افغانان afg̠ẖānān ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افغان (afġān).

def: s.m. A people (called also Paṭhāns), who inhabit the country to the N.W. of Lahore. (They pretend to derive their origin, as well as their name, from Afg̠ān, the reputed grandson of Malik Tālūt, or King Saul.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افغان (afġān).

ety: part. act. of afgandan, see afganda
def: adj. Casting, throwing down; casting forth (used in compn.).
more: part. in compos. (of افگندن) Casting, throwing down, casting forth.

r: afganda, afgandan, af, kantan, aibi, kan
ety: perf. part. of afgandan = af+Pehl. kantan = Zend aibi+rt. kan = S.अभि+rt. खन्
def: part. adj. Thrown, overthrown, cast forth.
s.f. Throwing, casting, overthrowing.
adj. Of or relating to the Afg̠āns; — s.f. The Afg̠ān language ( = Pushtū).
more: 1. adj. Of the Afghāns. 2. s. f. The being an Afghān.

def: (in compos.) s. f. Throwing, casting.
adj. Wounded; — s.m. A sore (on the back of a beast of burden).
adj. Abstinent, continent, chaste, virtuous, decent, modest, decorous; — ʻafīfa, s.f. A chaste woman, a modest woman, &c.

ety: for A. عفيفة, fem. of ʻafīf
def: adj. Abstinent, continent, chaste, virtuous, decent, modest, decorous; — ʻafīfa, s.f. A chaste woman, a modest woman, &c.
s.f. Opium.
more: n. f. S. अहिफेन See aphīm. afīm'-chī, afīm'an, afīmī , adj. Opium-eater; one addicted to opium; (Fig.) a sot. afīmī-chiṛā, lit . Opium bird. An opium-eater.
(corrup. of افیون) s. f. Opium. (Sans. modern अफेन, आफुक . Hunter ).

def: s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot
adj. Opium-eater; (fig.) a sot.
s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot
s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot
s.f. Praise, applause; — part. adj. & s.m. Creating; creator (used in compn. e.g. jahān-āfirīn, Creator of the world); — intj. Bravo! well done!
more: s. f. Praise, applause, acclamation. ¶ interject. Bravo! well done! part. (in compos.) Creating, creator; as جہان آفرین jahān-āfrīn, Creator of the world. آفرین کرنا āfrīn karnā, To praise, to applaud.
subs: (-par), To pronounce āfrīn (upon), to praise, laud, applaud.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آفرین (āfarīn), from Middle Persian 𐭠𐭯𐭥𐭩𐭭 (āfrīn, “praise, blessing”).

ety: for A. عافية, v.n. fr. عافا 'to grant health,' iii of عفو 'to efface,' &c.
def: s.f. Health, soundness; safety, security; well-being, welfare, freedom from evil or discomfort, &c.; success, prosperity.
more: n. f. عفو gave him health. Health; well-being. Qadr-i-āfiyat mālūm hogī . Prov. Saying. The value of health is felt after it is lost. āfiyat tang k. , v. a. To disturb one's comfort or repose. āfiyat-i-zātī . Personal safety. āfiyat-i-khalāyaq . Public tranquility; the public good. khair o āfyat pūchhā , v. a. To make kind enquiries after.
(from عفو Syr. To flourish) s. f. Health, safety.

def: s.f. pl. (of فکر fikr), Thoughts, meditations, opinions, counsels.
more: s. plur. (of فکر) Thoughts, meditations, opinions, counsels.

def: s.m. pl. (of فلك falak), The heavens, heavenly bodies; celestial orbs or spheres.
more: s. plur. (of فلک) The heavens, heavenly bodies, celestial orbs or spheres.

def: adj. Heavenly, celestial; relating to the heavenly bodies.
s.m. A person of overweening pride, an arrogant, disdainful man
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَفْلَاطُون (ʔaflāṭūn), from Ancient Greek Πλάτων (Plátōn).

def: s.m. pl. (of فرد fard), Single ones, single numbers, individuals; persons; sheets (of paper); separate sheets.
more: s. plur. (of فرد) Individuals, single ones, singular numbers, sheets (of paper).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افراد (afrād), plural form of فرد (fard), from Arabic فَرْد (fard, “individual”).

def: adj. & s.m. European, Frank; — a Frank, a native of Europe.
Name of an ancient king, who from Tartary invaded Persia, and was slain by Rustam.

ety: A. افراط ifrāt̤+تفريط tafrīt̤; root فرط
def: s.f. Hurry-skurry; confusion, disorder; bustle, tumult, commotion, uproar; alarm, consternation, panic; tumultuous dispersion, stampede.
more: Rus. n. f. Cor. of A. افراط تفريط 1. Uproar; tumult; disorder; confusion. Aisī afrā-tafrī thī, ki apnī jān kā bhī hosh na thā . Wom. 2. Alarm; fright; consternation; panic. Aisī afrā-tafrī huī, ki kisī kī surat na rahī . 3. Flight ( bhaggī ); retreat; stampede.

ety: imperat. of afrāḵẖtan = af+rāḵẖtan; Zend aibi+rt. rag; S. abhi+langh
def: part. adj. Exalting, raising; extolling (used in compn.).
more: part. act. in compos. (from افراختن) Exalting, raising.

ety: perf. part of āfrīdan; ā+rt. Zend frī = S. प्री
def: part. adj. Created; — s.m. Creature.
s.m. The Creator.
intj. Bravo! well done! ( shābāsh ). Āfrīṅ tujh ko! terī nāma-barī ko shābāsh! Zafar. āfrīṅ kar'nā,—paṛh'nā , v. a. To praise; applaud; pronounce a panegyric. Tumhāre kām par sab āfrīṅ karte haiṅ .
part. adj. & s.m. Creating; — the Creator.

ety: abst. s. fr. āfrīn; ish = Zend she
def: s.f. Creation.

ety: perf. part. of afroḵẖtan = af+roḵẖtan; Zend aibi+rt. ruć; S. abhi+ruć
def: part. adj. Set on fire, lighted, kindled, inflamed.
more: part. pass. (of افروختن) Inflamed, set on fire, lighted.

def: adj. & s.m. Kindling, illuminating, enlightening; inflaming; kindler, &c. (used in compn.).
more: part. act. in compos. (from افروختن) Illuminating, enlightening, kindling.

def: s.m. Gall (both the tree and the nut or fruit).
more: s. m. Gall (both the tree and the nut).

def: adj. compar. & superl. (of فصيح faṣīḥ), More, or most, eloquent; very eloquent; speaking very chastely or correctly.
more: adj. compar. (of فصیح) Very eloquent, speaking very correctly.

ety: af = Zend aiwi or aibi = S. abhi; sāna, fr. Zend rt. śā = S. śā (śo)
def: s.m. Tale, fiction, story, romance
more: n. m. A fiction; fable; romance ( kahānī ).
s. m. A fiction, tale, story, romance. افسانه خوان afsāna-ḵẖẉān, or افسانه گو afsāna-go, A story-teller, romance-teller.
subs: s.f. Story-telling; the profession of a story-teller.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَفْسَانَه (afsāna). Doublet of فَسانَہ (fasāna).

def: s.f. Wormwood, Artemisia absynthium.
more: n. f. Wormwood; Artemisia absinthium , or the Indian wormwood; Artemisia Indica . Wat.

def: s.m. Officer, official superior, one who exercises authority, a superior
more: n. m. 1. An officer; one who exercises authority. 2. A superior; official superior. afsar-i-ala ; H. P. baṛā afsar , n. m. Chief authority; the highest officer. afsar-i-bālā dast , n. m. Superior officer. afsar-i-parmaṭ , n. m. A Custom's officer.
s. m. f. A crown, a diadem.
subs: Superior officer, highest officer, chief authority
wety: Borrowed from English officer.

def: s.m.f. Crown, diadem.
wety: Borrowed from English officer.

ety: part. act. of afshāndan; af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+caus. of rt. Zend shu = S.च्यु
def: part. adj. Scattering, strewing, dispersing, shedding, pouring out (used in compn., e.g. gul-afshān, Strewing roses; zar-afshān kāg̠aẕ, Paper sprinkled with gold); — s.f. ( = zar afshān), Strips of gold and silver leaf or tinsel, or threads of muqqaish (q.v.) chipped very fine, pasted as ornaments on the forehead or the cheeks of women, or on books, letters, &c.
more: part. (of افشاندن) Scattering, strewing, shedding, dispersing, pouring out, scatterer, scattered, strewed, &c. (used in composition chiefly; as گل افشان gul-afshān, Strewing roses. زرافشان کاغذ zar-afshān-kāg̠ẖaẕ, Paper sprinkled with gold).
s. (dakh.) Shreds of muḳḳaish or cloth woven with gold or silver thread, chipped very fine, or slips of gold or silver leaf pasted with gum on the cheeks ( Qanoon-e-islam, p. 125).

def: n. f. Poet. Strips of tinsel pasted on a woman's forehead as ornaments. afshāṅ ho'nā , v. n. To be sprinkled with (color, spangles, etc.). afshānī kāgaz, zar afshānī kāgaz , n. m. Paper studded or spangled with gold leaf or dust, used in writing to persons of distinction. zar afshāṅ , n. m. Threads of muqqaish , or cloth woven with gold and silver thread, chipped very fine, or slips of gold pasted with gum on the Koran, letters, etc.
adj. Sprinkled, scattered over, &c.; — s.f. Scattering, sprinkling, dispersion
more: 1. adj. Sprinkled, scattered, dispersed. افساني کاغذ afshānī kāg̠ẖaẕ, Sprinkled (with gold, &c.) paper. 2. (In compos.) s. f. Scattering, sprinkling.

def: s. (dakh.) For آب شوره āb-shora, q. v. ( Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. xli).

ety: perf. part. of afshurdan; af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+Zend rt. khshar = S. kshar
def: part. adj. Pressed, squeezed out; filtered.
more: part. pass. (of افشردن) Pressed, squeezed out, filtered.

def: s.m. Sorrow, grief, concern; regret; vexation; — intj. Ah! alas! alack! ah me! what a pity!
more: s. m. Sorrow, concern, regret, vexation. ¶ interj. Ah! alas! افسوس کرنا afsos karnā, To sigh, to sorrow, to regret, grieve, lament, bewail, bemoan, mourn, moan.
subs: afsos karnā (-kā, or par), To feel regret, sorrow, &c., to regret; to grieve, sorrow, or sigh (for, or because of), to lament, bemoan; to take to heart; to express sorrow or regret (for); to feel or to express pity (for).
ex: اُس کو کچھ افسوس نہیں ہوا۔
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افسوس (afsōs).

def: s. f. (dakh.) Regret, repentance, grief, sorrow.
s.m. Incantation, charm, spell, verses used in spells or enchantments, fascination, sorcery, witchcraft
more: n. m. 1. Incantation; charm ( mantar ); spell; verses used in enchantment. 2. Fascination; witchery; sorcery; witchcraft; magic ( jādū ); the black art. afsūṅ chal'nā , v. n. 1. To be effective (a charm). 2. To produce an effect; to be effective; to win over. Karūṅ kyā, aë sajan, tujh par merā afsūṅ nahīṅ chaltā, Vagarnah, ek ishāreh sūṅ parī mujh ghar chalī āve . Walī. 3. To be conducive to; to succeed; gain one's end or object. afsūṅ karnā,—phūṅknā,—mārnā,—paṛhnā, —chalānā, jādū k . v. a. To enchant; use spells; exorcise; conjure.
s. m. Incantation, a spell, verses used in spells or enchantment, fascination, sorcery. افسون سازي afsūn-sāz-ī, s. f. Enchantment. افسون کرنا afsūn karnā, To enchant, to use spells or incantations.
subs: (-par), A charm to work or take effect (upon)
(-par), To practise charms or incantations (on), work spells (on), to enchant, to exorcise, to conjure: (afsūn is combined, in the same sense, with paṛhnā, phūṅknā, ćalānā and mārnā)
afsun-sāz, s.m. One who uses spells, enchanter, sorcerer, wizard, magician
afsūn-sāzī, s.f. Enchantment, using of spells; bewitching, fascination; sorcery, &c. ( = afsūn).

def: s. m. An enchanter, one who uses spells, &c., a sorcerer, magician.

ety: perf. part. of afsurdan; Zend aibi+śareta, rt. śar; S. abhi+rt. śar
def: part. adj. Frozen, frigid, benumbed; withered, faded; dispirited, dejected, low-spirited, melancholy
more: adj. Frozen, withered, faded, benummed; dejected, dispirited, lowspirited, melancholy. افسرده خاطر afsurdā-ḵẖāt̤ir, Depressed in mind, dispirited. افسرده دل afsurda-dil, Faint-hearted, lowspirited, dejected.
subs: adj. Depressed in mind, dispirited, afflicted — afsurda-dil, adj. Fainthearted, low-spirited, dejected.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افسرده.

def: s.f. Frozenness; frigidity, coldness; numbness; dejection, melancholy, lowness or depression of spirits.
more: s. f. Dejection, melancholy, lowness or depression of spirits.

def: s.m. (āf+tāb, q.q.v.), s.m. Lit. 'Sunshine'; the sun
more: n. m. The sun. āftāb-parast , n. m. A worshipper of the sun. āftab-parastī , n. f. Worship of the sun.
s. m. The sun, sunshine. آفتاب پرست āftāb-parast, A worshipper of the sun. آفتاب پرستي āftāb-parast-ī, s. f. Worship of the sun. آفتاب گیر āftāb-gīr, A parasol, an umbrella.
subs: s.m. A worshipper of the sun
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آفْتَاب (āftāb). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi آفتاب (āftab /⁠ãftāb⁠/).

def: s.m. An ewer, water-pot ( = āb-tāba, q.v.).
adj. Of the sun; like the sun; solar; faded by the sun, yellow; — s.f. A parasol of a particular form; a target studded with gold; a shield made of tortoise-shell; a kind of firework.
more: n. f. 1. A parasol of a particular form. 2. A target studded with gold; a shield made of tortoise-shell. 3. A kind of firework.
1. adj. Of the sun, solar, faded by the sun, yellow. 2. s. f. A parasol of a particular form, a target studded with gold.

def: s.m. = آفتابه āftābo, q.v.
more: s. m. An ewer. See آفتابه.

ety: for A. ʻafwuṉ, inf. n. of عفو 'to efface, blot out,' &c.
def: s.m. Absolution, forgiveness, pardon, remission (of sins)
more: n. m. عفو erased. Pardon; forgiveness; remission of sins. afū k. , v. a. To pardon ( bakhshnā , 2).
s. m. Absolution, forgiveness, pardon. عفو کرنا aﻌfū karnā, To pardon. عفو هونا aﻌfū honā, To be pardoned.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic عَفْو (ʕafw).

def: s.m. (pl. of فوه fūh), Lit. 'Mouths'; report, rumour, hearsay, notoriety, fame
more: plur. (of فوه) 1. s. m. Mouths. 2. s. f. Doubtful news. 3. Fame, report.
subs: To spread a report; to noise abroad.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَفْوَاه (ʔafwāh), plural of فَم (fam, “mouth”).

def: adj. Reported, rumoured, noised abroad, famed.
s.f. Naval forces, the navy
more: s. plur. (of فوج) Armies, crowds. افواج قاهره afwāj-i-ḳāhira, A victorious army.

def: s.f. = افيم afīm, q.v. s.f. Opium.
more: s. f. Opium, the inspissated juice of the Papaver somniferum. (The word may be derived from افن To deprive of reason or prudence ( Gol. ); or, rather from Gr. ΄οπος , Juice, especially of the Laserpitium ( Hippoc. ), whence ΄οπιον ( Dioscor. ); or, from the Sans. अहिफेन ahiphen, comp.of अहि Serpent, and फेन Froth, foam. Hunter ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَفْیُون (afyūn), from Arabic أَفْيُون (ʔafyūn), ultimately from Ancient Greek ὄπιον (ópion).

def: adj. & s.m. = افيمی afīmī, q.v. s.m. Opium-eater; (fig.) A sot

ety: imperat. & v.n. of afzūdan; see afzūd
def: adj. Increasing (used in compn.).
more: part. act. (of افزودن) in compos. Increasing.

ety: imperat. & v.n. of afzūdan; see afzūd
def: adj. Increasing (used in compn.).
(in compos.) s. f. Increasing.
s.f. Act of increasing; increase, addition.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افزایش (afzāyiš).

def: n. f. Increase; addition ( baṛhotrī ).
adj. compar. & superl. (of فاضل fāẓil), More excellent; most excellent; preëminent; best; highest
more: adj. compar. (of فاضل) More or most excellent, preeminent.

def: s.m. pl. (of فضل faẓl), Favours; virtues, graces, accomplishments.
more: s. plur. (of فضل) Graces, virtues, favours. ¶ ifẓāl (inf. iv. of فضل) s. Making to excel or increase, benefitting, grace (divine).

def: s.f. Excellence, preëminence.

ety: afzā, q.v. anda = Zend añṭ = S. anta
def: part. adj. Increasing; that which increases (used in compn.).
more: part. act. (of افزایدن) in compos. Increasing.

ety: v.n. of afzūdan = Zend aiwi+rt. śu; S. abhi+śu
def: s.m. Increase, addition, enlargement; abundance; — adj. More, greater, additional, superfluous, in excess
more: n. m. Increase; enlargement; abundance; supernumerary ( bauhtāt ). afzūṅ' ho'nā , v. n. Ped. for zyādah honā . 1. To increase; enlarge ( baṛhnā ). 2. To rise in price ( bhāo chaṛhnā ).
s. m. Increase, addition, abundance. adj. More, greater, additional.
subs: (-se), To be more (than), to exceed, surpass, &c.

ety: abst. s. fr. afzūda, perf. part. of afzūdan
def: s.f. Increase, addition.
adj. Increasing; more; greater; manifold, much
more: adj. Increasing, more, greater, manifold, much.
subs: v.n. To increase, enlarge; to rise (in price).

def: s.f. Increase, enlargement; abundance; rise (in price).
s.m. = آك āk, q.v. s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
more: n. f. S. अग्नि, Pr. &. Sah. ag'gi . 1. Fire; flame ( lau ). 1. Mere hī se āg lāī, nām dharā basandar . Prov. She got the fire from me, and she calls it "Sacred Fire." ☞ When a return of the favour is asked of her, she says it is basandar (sacred fire) which it is not lawful to give away. 2. Do ādmȳoṅ ke bīch meṅ se āg na nikālo, nahiṅ to un meṅ laṛāī hogī . Majālis-un-nisā. Dont take up the fire while two persons are standing on either side of it, or there will be a quarrel between them. 3. Āg khāëgā, so aṅgāre hagegā . Prov. 2. Warmth; heat; excessive heat of the sun; a very hot season. 3. Used metaphorically for anger ( tehā ); love ( ishq ); lust ( jhal ); jealousy ( jalan ); hatred ( lāg ); great hunger ( khuddyā ). 1. Baṛī āg-bharī lugāī haï, be-laṛe chain nahīṅ . 2. Peṭ kī āg . Maternal love. 3. Peṛū kī āg . Lust. 4. Venereal disease; syphilis. āg uṭhā'nā , v. a. To create or caus…

adj. 1. Very hot; warm; hot as fire ( tattā ). 2. Fiery; fuming; violent; hot-tempered. 3. Waspish; peppery; irritable; irascible. 4. Sharp; acute; quick. Āg the ibtidā-i-ishq meṅ ham, Ho gaë khāk, intihā hai yeh . Mukhaivar. 5. Scarce and dear; high priced ( āg ke mol ). Kanāgatoṅ meṅ dūd dahī to āg hotā hai. āg-boṭ, ag'an-boṭ ; Ped. dūd-kash ; Pop. dhuāṅ-kash , n. m. A steamer; a steam-boat.

s. f. Fire. آگ اٿهانا āg uṭhānā, v. a. To raise disturbance, to enrage, to provoke. آگ بجهانا āg bujhānā, v. a. To extinguish fire; to appease a tumult, to pacify a quarrel, to still resentment. آگ برسنا āg barasnā, v. n. To rain fire (applied to the extreme heat of the sun, or to a hot fire of cannon or musketry in battle). آگ بڙنا āg baṛnā, v. n. 1. To be enraged. 2. To be very hungry. آگ بهکنا āg bhaknā or آگ پهانکنا āg phānknā, v. a. To speak idle words, to boast. آگ پڙنا āg paṛnā, v. n. To be enraged; as, میري باتون سي اس پر آگ پڙتي هي merī bātoṅ se us par āg paṛtī hai, He is enraged at my words: also, the same as آگ برسنا āg barasnā. آگ دینا āg denā, v. a. To burn a Hindū corpse. آگ سلگانا āg sulgānā, v. a. To inflame; to excite sedition, to foment a quarrel clandestinely. آگ کرنا āg karnā, v. a. 1. To make any thing exceedingly warm. 2. To excite envy or anger (used among women). …
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀺 (aggi), from Sanskrit अग्नि (agní), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hagníš, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁n̥gʷnis. Doublet of اَگَن (agan) (semi-learned) and اَگْنِی (agnī) (learned).

def: s.f. Fire; flame; heat, excessive heat; (met.) anger, passion; love; lust; hatred; jealousy; intense hunger; venereal disease, syphilis; — adj. Hot as fire, very hot; fiery, hot-tempered, irritable; sharp, quick; scarce, dear
subs: āg uṭhā-rakhnā (-meṅ), To create or cause a quarrel (among or between), to raise a disturbance; to enrage, provoke
v.n. To be exceedingly enraged ( = āg honā)
(-kī), To extinguish fire; to quench the thirst (of); to satisfy hunger, lust, appetite, &c.; to appease a tumult, pacify a quarrel, still resentment

(-par), To rain fire (upon), to pour in shot and shell, to bombard, cannonade; — āg barasnā, v.n. To be very hot; to be parched up; (hence) to become scarce and dear (fire, as it were, raining upon, -par, a thing, and parching it, and so, causing a scarcity); falling in abundance of shot and shell
(-kī), To have the fire of anger, hunger, &c. kindled, to be enraged; to be hungry, &c.
(or babūlā) honā, v.n. To be in a whirlwind of passion, to be mad with rage

To eat fire; to exaggerate greatly; to fabricate, invent; to lie grossly; to boast enormously
Antagonism (such as that) of fire and water; natural antagonism; mortal enmity
v.n. To be annoyed (by), enraged (at); to feel the burning (of heat, grief, hunger, &c.), to be very hot, &c. = āg barasnā, q.v.
v.n. To be blown into a flame; to feel great heat; to be greatly excited, be worked into a fury, get into a towering passion
Antagonism (like that) of fire and straw, &c. = āg-pānī kā bair, q.v.
v.t. To blow the fire, fan the flame; to excite, inflame; to burn excessively, feel on fire
To throw water on the fire, to extinguish the fire; to quench thirst, &c. = āg bujhānā, q.v.
To roll on fire; to be uneasy or restless; to be consumed with jealousy, envy, &c.
To shake off the ashes from a live coal; to strike fire (from a flint, &c.)
v.t. To set fire (to), apply a light or torch (to); to burn, consume, destroy

To kindle or make a fire; to inflame, excite sedition, foment a quarrel
v.n. To recover from a violent fit of temper, to cool down
s.m. An extremely active and energetic person; a man of quick apprehension, a sharp fellow
s.m. A spark of fire; a piece of fire, a burning coal
To light or make a fire; to make (a thing) exceedingly warm; to excite anger or envy (com. used among women)
(-meṅ), To sow discord, &c., see āg lagānā
Lit. 'To run for water after setting fire to'; to make a show of curing an evil caused by ooneself; to pretend to appease a quarrel which one has purposely excited; to play tricks, deceive

(-ko, or -meṅ), To apply fire (to), set on fire; to burn, consume; to kindle the fire of strife, sow dissension, make mischief, cause a quarrel, create a disturbance; to inflame, excite, enrage; to eat up, consume; to squander, waste, ruin, destroy; to occasion loss (to); to reduce to poverty; to make a grand display, to do wonders; to treat as worthless or vile, to cast aside, throw or put away, abandon; to discard, disown; to treat with scorn, to scorn, despise; to pay too high a price, to buy dear; to swindle, cheat, pilfer
adj. & s.m. Combustible, inflammable; an incendiary; a seditious person, firebrand, instigator of quarrels

or lag-jānā, v.n. To be set on fire, to be on fire; to catch fire; to burn, be hot; to smart (as a wound); to burn with rage, indignation, envy, jealousy, &c., to be enraged or incensed; to be consumed with love, or grief; to feel intense hunger; to perish, wither, be blighted; to become scarce and dear
intj. May it be burned! perish! blast thee! a murrain seize thee! plague take it!

Lit. 'To look for a cat's urine when (the house) is on fire': to go in quest of small and rarely-found remedies to meet a present and great danger; to put off, delay or excuse oneself on vain pretences
To dig a well after fire has broken out; to shut the stable-door after the horse is stolen
Lit. 'To come for fire'; to pay a flying visit, to pay a doctor's visit

To become fire; to become very hot; to take fire, to burn with rage, be enraged.

def: s.m. Front, fore-part, fore-ground; forehead; countenance, face; the private parts; space in front of a house; fore-part of a turband; head or fore-part of a ship or boat; van, vanguard; first charge or assault, onset; that which is gone before, the past (time); that which is before, the future, the morrow, hereafter
more: n. m. S. अग्र before. 1. The front; forepart; foreground. 2. The front (of the body); the forehead; countenance; face; appearance. 3. The private parts of the body. 1. Dil meṅ kisī ke hargiz, naë sharm, naë hayā hai. Āgā bhī khal rahā hai, pīchhā bhī khul rahā hai. Bālpan . Childhood. Nazīr. No shame, no blush upon the cheek you find, Open before, and open too behind. 2. Zāhir bhī unkā sāf hai. jiuṛā bhī sāf hai, Āgā bhī unkā sāf hai, pīchhā bhī sāf hai. Hījṛe . Eunuchs. Nazīr. Fair frontispiece, and fair in mind, Both fair before, and fair behind. 4. The front part of a turban or coat. Araz meṅ āgā banā lo . 5. The prow; the head or forepart of a ship. 6. The van; vanguard; advanced guard. 7. The first charge or assualt; the onset. 8. ( agat ) The future; futurity; the time to come; the morrow; hereafter. āgā bhārī ho nā , v. n. lit . To be heavy in front. 1. To be pregnant; to be in th…
s. m. The front, the forepart; the space in front of a house, the forepart of a turband. آگا پیچها کرنا āgā pīchhā karnā, v. n. To hesitate, waver, boggle, demur, stand shill-I-shall-I.
subs: Lit. 'To be heavy in the front'; to be pregnant; to be rough, uneven or dangerous (a road)
s.m. Front and stern, the private parts (of the body); the cloth forming the fore and back part of a jāma from the neck to the waist; hesitation, vacillation
To look before and behind; to be cautious, to take care, consider well
To consider well the past and the future, &c., to think about, reflect, deliberate
(-kā), To go after (one), hang about (one), dog one's steps; to hesitate, waver, vacillate, boggle, demur, stand shilly-shally
(-kā), To look after, attend to; to question; to call to account
āgā lenā (-kā), To go forwards, to proceed, advance; to come forward; to take the lead; to bark (a dog) on the approach of a stranger
(-kā), To attack or strike in front; to attack the vanguard (of an army).

s.f. Future, futurity, the hereafter ( = āgā, q.v.). s.m. Lord, chief, master ( = آقا āqā); a Mog̠al, — generally a Mog̠al trader from Kābul.
part. adj. Arrived, come; present; — s.m. A guest.
more: n. f. 1. Property; wealth; stock ( pūṅjī ). 2. Respect; courtesy; attention. agaitī khetī , n. f. An early crop, in contradistinction to ( pachhaitī late).

def: s.f. Income; wealth, stock, property; respect, courtesy, attention, consideration (see next).
s.m. A butcher's stall. (The word appears to be peculiar to Southern India.)
s.f. Arrival; concern.

adj. Without character, dishonoured, disgraced; — s.m.f. One whose funeral ceremonies have not been performed; — s.f. Distress, want of resource, necessity, inconvenience; disgrace, dishonour; condemnation, damnation.
v.t. To bring together, collect, assemble.
adj. Bodiless, incorporeal.
adj. Coming, arriving, approaching; future.
s.m. Concern.
s.m. Advance, &c. (see āga and agāṛī); — adv. In advance, in front, before, beforehand
subs: v.n. To advance in order to meet a person coming on a visit.

def: adv. Before, in advance, beforehand: see آگي āge.
v. n. To advance to meet a person.
s.m. The top of the sugar-cane cut up for seed.

s.f. = agwānī, q.v. s.m. Guide, leader, fore-runner, harbinger ( = agwā); agent, manager; — s.f. Guidance, direction, leadership; reception, welcome, going or sending forward to meet and receive a visitor with honour ( = agaunī; syn. istiqbāl)
subs: (-kī), To advance to meet the bridegroom at a wedding, or a visitor on the road; to escort to one's home.

def: v. a. To advance to meet the bridegroom, to go to meet a visitant on the road.
s.m. An advance of rent paid by the cultivator to the zamīndār in the months of jeṭh and āsāṛh (cf. agāṛī)
more: E. adv. An advance of rent paid by the cultivator to the Zamīndār in the months of Jeṭh and Āsāṛh. agaur baṭāī , n. f. A division of the crop in predeterminated proportions between landlord and tenant; a watching and sharing, each party keeping a watch over the fields that none of the crop be fraudulently made away with.
subs: s.f. A division of the crop in predetermined proportions between landlord and tenant; a watching and sharing, each party keeping watch (see next) over the fields to prevent the crop being fraudulently carried off.

def: s.f. = agaur, q.v. s.m. An advance of rent paid by the cultivator to the zamīndār in the months of jeṭh and āsāṛh (cf. agāṛī)
subs: s.f. A division of the crop in predetermined proportions between landlord and tenant; a watching and sharing, each party keeping watch (see next) over the fields to prevent the crop being fraudulently carried off.

def: s.m. Beginning, commencement
more: n. m. Beginning; commencement ( shurū ). āgāz se anjām tak ; H. ād se ant tak , q. v. āgāz kar'nā , v. n. To take the first step; to begin ( shurū k .). āgāz ho'nā , v. n. To commence; to begin to be ( shurū honā ).
subs: v.t. To make a beginning, to begin
v.n. To commence, begin to be.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آغَاز (āġāz).

def: s.m. = اگڙا agṛā, q.v. adj. Front, foremost; first, prior, anterior; chief, prominent, best; — s.m. Foremost point or part, forepart, front, tip, uppermost part
subs: s.m. The first, chief, front or foremost part of anything
adj. Of prior birth, firstborn; — s.m. An elder brother
adj. Preceding; s.m. Chief, leader, guide
adj. Forecasting, provident, cautious; — s.m. One endued with foresight; a cautious man
s.f. The advance guard of an army
s.m. The eighth month in the lunar year of the Hindūs (Nov.-Dec.), when the moon is full near the head of Orion.

def: adj. Firm, strong; resolute, brave, unflinching.
adj. From H. gaḍā, gaṛā fixed, rooted. Brave; firm; resolute; unflinching; strong.
more: n. m. An ear of corn or rice which has been blighted and contains no grain.

def: intj. (used to a female of the lower order) O! holla! (the word appears to be peculiar to Southern and Western India).

adv. ( = agāṛī and āgū), Before, in front, in the presence of, confronting, facing, opposite, in view, in sight; in the time or reign; in advance; foremost; fore, beyond, onward, further, further on, furthermore, more than this; in future, hereafter, henceforth; again; for the future; next in time or place, then, afterwards; thereupon, after that; formerly, in former times; already
more: adv. 1. Before; in front; in the presence of. 1. Mat kar sās burāī tere bhī āge jāī . Prov. The ill you do to me to-day, Tomorrow will your daughter pay. (The remonstrance of the daughter-in-law with her mother-in-law). 2. Āge dauṛ, pīchhe chauṛ . Prov. Fast run, fast lo-e. (The more haste the worse speed). 3. Āge nāth, na pīchhe pagā, Sab se bhalā kumhār kā gadhā . Prov. Nor halter, nor heel-ropes has he, As the potter's donkey who so free! 2 Opposite; vis-a-vis ; fronting; facing. 3. In view; in sight; before one's nose. Apnī chīz apne āge bhali . 4. In the life time, or reign of. 5. In advance; foremost; headmost; ahead. Dauṛ meṅ āge nikal gayā . 6. Hereafter; henceforth: in future; after this. 7. Again; over again; for the future. 8. Next in time or place; then; afterwards. 9. Beyond; further on; further; further more; more than this. Sāiṅ āge dekho . Holy father, ask further on. 10.…
subs: adv. (intensive or emphat. of āge), in advance, before, ahead; later on, by and by, hereafter

v.n. To come forward, come in sight; to advance, approach, draw near; to confront, face, defy, challenge; to come between, interpose; to come to pass, happen, occur, befall, betide; to come upon (retributively); to return, revert; to reap what one has sown; to come to light, be discovered

v n. To go before, advance, go forward or ahead; to make head or progress, push on, get along; to go forward to meet and escort a visitor; to go beyond, surpass, outstrip, excel; to come forward, face, confront, oppose; to challenge; to lead, show the way

adv. One after another, successively, in succession, in a line; before and behind, front and rear, fore and aft; again, hereafter, henceforth, for the future; sooner or later; in the absence of, behind one's back; on fitting occasion or opportunity; straggling, irregularly; in inverted order, transposed
v.t. To place, lay or put before; to present, offer; to make (one) go before, keep (one) in sight; to put forward
adv. Looking before; with the eyes open; carefully, with circumspection
v.n. To look before, keep the eyes open, look about one, take care, exercise caution or circumspection; to look to the future
v.t. To throw or place before; to supply with the means of support
adv. Before-hand, in anticipation, previously, before: (in phrases it may often be conveniently rendered by the prefix pre-, as āge-se ṭhānnā, To predetermine, presume, preconceive
( = agāṛū rakhnā), v.t. To put forward; to place or lay before, &c. = āge dhar-lenā, q.v.
s.m. Dependant, follower, servant; parasite, hanger-on, sycophant
v.t. To bring forward, adduce, produce; to place, lay or set before
adv. Further on (in time or space), in future; ahead, subsequently
v.t. To bring forward, advance, to bring before
v.n. To go before, advance, &c. ( = āge baṛhnā); to be forward or bold; to face (one) boldly, to be rude or impertinent.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa अग्गइ (aggaï), from Sanskrit अग्रके (agrake).

def: interj. (dakh.) O! (used to a female).
s.f. = angeṭhī, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) A furnace (as, of a blacksmith), a grate (for fire), stove ( Harris ): see ٿهيپ ṭhīp.

def: s.m.f. Bar (of a door or window), bolt.
more: W. Rus. n. f. S. अर्गल , Pālī aggalo . A bar for fastening a door.
s. (dakh.) Bar (of a door), bolt ( Harris ).

def: s.m. A race of merchants of the Vaisya tribe (from Agrohā, to the west of Dehlī); — the descendants of Rājā Agar.
s.m. The first morsel.
s.f. Command, order, injunction, precept, instruction, direction; provision, requisition; permission, leave, authority; edict, decree; (in Gram.) the imperative mood
subs: (-kī), To observe or obey the orders (of)
s.m. Written order or authority, warrant, commission, edict
aggyā-kārī, adj. Executing command, acting according to orders; obedient, submissive, dutiful
v.t. To give, or issue, an order (to, -ko), to command, bid, instruct, direct; to permit, allow; to decide, settle; to do the bidding (of, -kī), to obey, submit (to)
v.t. To keep under authority, subjection, or control; to rule, govern
(-kī), To be under the order or control or authority (of), to be subject (to), to obey, submit (to); to be docile
v.t. 'To bring under authority, control or subjection, to subdue, master
(S. ājnā+anusāra), adv. & postpn. In accordance with the command or bidding (of, -kī).

def: s.f. Command, order, injunction, precept, instruction, direction; provision, requisition; permission, leave, authority; edict, decree; (in Gram.) the imperative mood
more: n. f. S. आ + ज्ञा to command. 1. Order; command; injunction; precept. 2. Provision; direction; instruction; requisition. 3. Leave; license; warrant; permission. 4. An edict; award; decree. 5. In Gram . The imperative mood. āggyā pāl'nā , v. n. To obey orders ( hukm bajā lānā ). aggyā de'nā, aggyā kar'nā , v. a. 1. To issue or pass an order; give orders. 2. To permit; allow; admit. 3. To decide; settle; conclude. 4. To instruct; direct; guide. ag'gyā-kā'rī , adj. 1. Acting according to orders. 2. Obedient; submissive ( tābe-dār ); dutiful. So woh jag meṅ dhan hai nārī, Jo prītam kī aggyā-kōrī. Chaupāī . All praise unto the duteous wife Who minds her husband all her life. aggyā meṅ rakh'nā , v. a. 1. To keep under subjection or control. 2. To govern; rule. āggyā meṅ rah'nā, āggyā mān'nā , v. n. To obey; submit. aggyā meṅ lā'nā , v. a. To bring under control; subdue; master.
subs: (-kī), To observe or obey the orders (of)
s.m. Written order or authority, warrant, commission, edict
aggyā-kārī, adj. Executing command, acting according to orders; obedient, submissive, dutiful
v.t. To give, or issue, an order (to, -ko), to command, bid, instruct, direct; to permit, allow; to decide, settle; to do the bidding (of, -kī), to obey, submit (to)
v.t. To keep under authority, subjection, or control; to rule, govern
(-kī), To be under the order or control or authority (of), to be subject (to), to obey, submit (to); to be docile
v.t. 'To bring under authority, control or subjection, to subdue, master
(S. ājnā+anusāra), adv. & postpn. In accordance with the command or bidding (of, -kī).

def: adj. Ignorant, inexperienced, unknowing, foolish, stupid, idiotic; unknown; — adv. Unwittingly, &c.; — s.m. An ignorant person, &c.; — a stranger.
adj. Acting according to order, obedient.
s.m. Ignorance foolishness, folly, stupidity
more: n. m. S. अ not, ज्ञा to know. Ignorance; folly; stupidity. aggyā'nī, ajān, an-jān ; P. nā'dān , adj. 1. Not knowing; unknowing; ignorant. 2. Thoughtless; inconsiderate; foolish.
adj. Witless, unknowing, ignorant, not knowing what to do; a simpleton.
wety: From Sanskrit अज्ञान (ajñāna).

def: adj. Not knowing, unknowing, ignorant, inexperienced; thoughtless, inconsiderate, foolish; — s.m. An ignorant person, &c.
(S. ajnānatwam), s.m. = H اگيانتا अज्ञानता aggyāntā (S. ajnānatā),s.f. Ignorance, folly, &c. ( = aggyān).
more: s. m. Ignorance, foolishness, stupidity, simplicity.

def: (S. ajnānatā), s.f. = H اگيانپن अज्ञानपन aggyānpan (S. ajnānatwam), s.m. Ignorance, folly, &c. ( = aggyān).
n. m. A written order; a warrant or commission; an edict.
part. adj. Unknown, not understood; ignorant, inexperienced, simple
subs: (S. ajnāta+yauvanā), s.f. A girl arrived at maturity and surprised at the symptoms which then appear, not knowing to what to attribute her sensations.

def: s.f. Ignorance, &c. ( = aggyān).
s.m. Sin, guilt, crime, wickedness; suffering
subs: adj. & s.m. Sin-destroying, freeing from sin, purifying; — destroyer of sin
s.m. Remover of guilt, — an epithet of the Deity.

def: s.f. Surfeit, satiety, repletion, fulness; — adv. To the full or utmost; severely, harshly, with a strong or heavy hand.
adj. Satiated, surfeited, satisfied, full; — s.m. A rich man, one who enjoys a competency.
n. f. Fullness; repletion; surfeit; satiety; aghā'yā aghā'ī , W.; aghā'nā E. n. m. One who enjoys a competency; a rich man or woman.
more: adv. Severely; harshly; with a strong or heavy hand. E. rānī jī, abhī karūṅ darbār, sazā maiṅ dūṅ aghāī, Zulam mahal meṅ kȳā, kasar kuchh chhoṛī nāhīṅ . Play. Rūp Basaṅt .

def: s. (dakh.) Bar (of a door, Harris ): see H. اگل agal.
s. (dakh.) See آگم ā-gam.
s.m. The name of the eighth month of the luni-solar year of the Hindūs (Nov.-Dec.).
more: n. m. S. अग्रहायन, from अग्र before, हायन् year, i. e. the beginning of the year, Old style. The ninth month of the luni-solar year corresponding with November-December.
s. m. Name of the Hindū eighth month (during which the sun is in Scorpio and the moon is full near Orionʼs head), November-December.

def: v.n. To be surfeited or satiated (with, -se), to be full, satisfied, contented; to swell with pride, give oneself airs
more: v. n. 1. To be full ( apharnā ), surfeited, satisfied, satiated, gorged; to have enough. 1. Aisan buṛbak kaun haiṅ, khāt jo nahīṅ aghāë? E. Rus. Prov. Saying. Every fool knows when his stomach is full. 2. Eka hī sādhe sab sadhe, sab sādhe sab jāe. Jo gahi sewe mūl kūṅ, phūle, phale aghāe . Tulsī Dās. Attached to one, shall all obtain, To all given up, not one shall gain. Embrace the root and cherish it, Fruit-bending boughs your benefit. 3. Bhūkal ahas, ki ahas aghāë? Kahe to kuchh deoṅ khilāë . Bhoj. Birhā Are you hungry or full? Will you have any thing to eat? 2. To swell with pride; to give oneself airs. Khānā, aur aghānā . Prov. 1. Laid in, laid down. 2. To be full is to swell. aghā'nā E.; aghā'yā, aghā'ī , W. adj. Full; surfeited; satiated; satisfied. 1. Aghānā bagulā, poṭhyā tīt . Prov. The full stork smells stinking fish. (To a full belly all meat is bad). 2. Bhūkhe ber, aghāë gāṅ…
1. v. n. To surfeit, to be satiated. 2. adj. Satiated.

def: adj. Relating to the month Aghan; — s.f. The produce of that portion of the ḵẖarīf crop which is gathered in the month Aghan, the harvest of the cold season
more: E. n. f. The produce of that portion of the Kharīf , or cold weather crop, which is gathered in the month Aghan ; the harvest of the cold season. agh'nī fasl , n. The cold weather harvest, reaped in Nov. and Dec.

def: s.m. Greediness, covetousness, avarice.
more: s. m. f. (dakh.) Desire: see حرص ḥirṣ.

def: s.m. Placing sacred food, or sprinkling clarified butter, before an idol; sprinkling ghī upon the fire at certain sacrifices; ghī or clarified butter.
s.m. The plant Achyranthes aspera (said to cure the bite of venomous reptiles; — syn. ćirćirā).
n. m. A plant said to cure the bite of venomous reptiles.
adj. Greedy, covetous, avaricious (cf. aghorī); s.m. A miser.
more: adj. 1. Uncovered; exposed; undressed; naked; nude; bare. 1. Jā ke kāran pahrī sāṛhī, wohī ṭāṅg rahī ughāṛī . Wom. Prov. She exposed her legs after she had made a skirt to cover them. (All her labor was labor lost). 2. Le kar chīr kadam chaḍhi baiṭhe, maiṅ jal bīch ughāṛī . Song. Krishṅ Līlā . With all my clothes he made off up a tree, Me naked in the water standing see. 2. Plain; clear; manifest; evident.

def: s. f. (dakh.) Front, fore, in front: see اگاڙي agāṛī.
s.m. Land held in perpetuity and not alienable.
s.m. Beating; stroke, blow; cast, throw; gust; killing, slaughter; place of execution, slaughter-house.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit आघात (āghāta).

def: s.m. Beating; stroke, blow; cast, throw; gust; killing, slaughter; place of execution, slaughter-house.
adj. Not wounded, unscathed, sound, whole.
adj. Unfit, unworthy; — s.m. The unknown future.
adj. Satiated, surfeited, satisfied, full; — s.m. A rich man, one who enjoys a competency.
s.m. Lemon grass (see agyā-ghās, s.v. agyā).
s.m. A title of the god Śiva; — adj. Terrible, formidable
subs: s.m. An order of religious mendicants, among Hindūs, who worship Śiva: (They eat anything and everything, however filthy or unclean, even human carcases; — hence) a gross or filthy feeder; a dirty-looking man
s.m. A member of the order aghor-panth; glutton, &c. ( = the next, q.v.).

def: adj. & s.m. ( = aghor-panthī), Filthy, foul, impure, unclean; — a glutton, gormandizer.
more: adj. 1. Filthy; unclean; impure: nasty. 2. A glutton; greedy-gut; gormandizer; voracious eater. aghorī-pan,—panā , n. m. 1. Impurity; uncleanliness; filthiness; dirtiness. 2. Greediness; covetousness.
adj. (used substantively) An order of religious mendicants professing اگهورپنتهہ aghor-panth; (they eat every thing, however filthy, even human carcasses; hence) a gross or filthy feeder.
adj. (dakh.) Covetous, insatiable, greedy, voracious, eager; glutton, gormandizer. اگهوري پنا aghorī-panā, s. m. Greediness, covetousness, voracity, gluttony.

def: s.m. Filthiness, foulness, uncleanness, impurity; greediness, covetousness.
s.m. Filthiness, foulness, uncleanness, impurity; greediness, covetousness.
s. m. A particular religious order among Hindūs. See اگهورپنتهي.

n. m. aghor , a title of Shiv. from S. a not, ghor fear. An order of Hindū mendicants who worship Siv. They eat everything, however filthy or unclean, even human carcases; hence, a gross or filthy feeder; a dirty-looking man.
adj. (used substantively) An order of religious mendicants professing اگهورپنتهہ aghor-panth; (they eat every thing, however filthy, even human carcasses; hence) a gross or filthy feeder.
adj. Without noise or sound; (in Gram.) hard.
s.f. Fire, &c. ( = āg, q.v.) s.m. = آك āk, q.v. s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
more: adv. Before, in front, fore, beyond, more, ago, already, formerly, forwards, farther, onward, henceforward, along; in what is before or to be done, then, hence, rather, sooner. آگي آنا āge ānā, v. n. 1. To come forward, to advance. 2. To return, to revert. آگي دهرلینا āge dhar lenā, To get before, to outstrip, to surpass, to pass (as a horse in a race). آگي لانا āge lānā, To advance (actively), to bring forward.

def: adj. Future; possible. (Cf. aglā.)
s.m. Enclosure for sheep, sheepcote.
adj. Full (used in comp.)
s.m. Name of a bird, a species of lark ( = agiyā, q.v.). s.f. Fire, &c. = āg, q.v.
more: s. m. Name of a bird, a sort of lark: see اگیا agiyā.
subs: s.m. A demon who rules over fire
s.f. Lemon-grass, Andropogon schœnanthus (also called aghin- or agin-ghās).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit अग्नि (agni, “fire”). Doublet of آگ (āg). Compare Bengali আগুন (agun).

def: s. f. The farcy in horses, an eruptive disease in men.
s.m. Name of a bird, a species of lark, Alauda aggia (also called agin).
n. m. A demon who rules over fire. agiyā baitāl , adj. Quick; smart; sharp; knowing.
s.f. Fire, &c. = āg, q.v.
more: n. f. S. अग्नि H. āg , fire. A disease affecting plants, so that the plant seems burnt up (?).
n. m. A bird; Alauda aggia, Buch .
s. m. Name of a bird. (Alauda aggia, Buch. ).
subs: s.m. A demon who rules over fire
s.f. Lemon-grass, Andropogon schœnanthus (also called aghin- or agin-ghās).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Foremost, first, chief, principal; prior, previous, preceding, former, anterior; late, recent, belonging to the past, old; coming, approaching, next, subsequent, forthcoming, future; — s.m. The first, the foremost (person or thing), the chief, the best; the preceding, hence, (in the pl. agle), elders, ancients, forefathers, ancestors, predecessors; the living, the present generation; posterity, descendants, succeeding generations; another person, a second person ( = dūsrā, but generally implying a senior or a superior); husband, partner (husband or wife); the Supreme Being; supreme lord, tyrant, oppressor; the opposite party, opponent, adversary (in a lawsuit, or a match); the privities
more: adj. S. अग्र, H. āge foremost. 1. Coming; next; approaching; impending; at hand; forthcoming; that will be. Phr On the cards; all in good time. 2. Previous; preceding; anterior; foregoing. 3. First; foremost; chief; principal. Aglī bhaili pachhili, pachhili, pardhān . E Prov. The first became last, and the last, first. 4. Late; recent; former; old; belonging to a past age or time. aglā janam , n. m. Hin. 1. Former birth or life. 2. The next world; the future. aglī pichhlī kar'nī , n. f. The fruits of one's acts in this life, or in a former life according to the Hindu belief in the transmigration of souls. agle waqtoṅ se , adv. From past times; long ago; from a long time; from time immemorial. Agle waqtoṅ se yoṅ hī hotī āī hai. aglī, yā agāū mitī rakh'nā , v. a. To antedate.

n. m. 1. The first, or foremost person or thing. 2. A second person; another party. Aglā kahtā ho to āp chupkā ho rahiye . Prov. 3. The persons now living; the present generation; contemporaries. Agle ko ghās, na pichhle ko pānī . Prov. Nor grass for the living, nor water for the dead. 4. Elders; forefathers; ancestors; predecessors. 5. Deseendants; posterity; successors. 6. A husband (Wom); consort; wife. 1. Aglā ghar meṅ mahīṅ hai . Wom. 2. Aë mere agle, man māne so kar le . E. Wom. Prov. 7. A member of the wife's or husband's family. 8. God; the Supreme Being. Agle ke hāth bāt hai, chāhe to ab mehr kar de . 9. One side or party in a match, or lawsuit; an opponent; adversary; antagonist. 10. A despot; tyrant; oppressor. Aglā māre, aur rone na de . Prov. The tyrant smites, nor cry permits. 11. The private parts of the body. 12. Opp. of pichhlā . Sunset; evening; the time for breaking a …
adj. 1. Prior, preceding, first, foremost, former, chief, principal. 2. Ancestor, ancient. 3. Other, next.
subs: s.m. Former birth or life; the future, the next world
s.f. The fruits of one's acts in this life, or in a former existence, according to Hindū belief
pron. They, those; — adv. Formerly, previously ( = S. agre, and H. āge)
s.m. Ancestors, elders, predecessors; descendants, posterity; preceding and succeeding generations
adj. Belonging to a former age or generation; simple, artless, innocent, guileless, plain, straight-forward.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀮 (aggala), from Sanskrit अग्र (ágra, “front; top”) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀮- (-la-).

def: n. m. The foremast sail of a ship.
n. The foremast of a ship.
adj. & adv. (compar. & superl. of غالب g̠ālib), More, or most, likely; most probable; very probably
more: adj. compar. (of غالب) Superiour, stronger. ¶ adv. Most likely.
subs: It is most, or very probable (that, -ki).

def: s.m. pl. (of غل g̠ull), Yokes, chains (for the neck).
more: s. plur. (of غل g̠ẖull ) Yokes, chains.

def: s.m. The advanced guard; the East; — adj. Adventurous, venturesome; forward, early (as fruit, &c.).
more: s. m. The advanced guard. adj. Adventurous, venturesome, forward, early (as fruit).

def: s.f. = agwānī, q.v. s.m. Guide, leader, fore-runner, harbinger ( = agwā); agent, manager; — s.f. Guidance, direction, leadership; reception, welcome, going or sending forward to meet and receive a visitor with honour ( = agaunī; syn. istiqbāl)
subs: (-kī), To advance to meet the bridegroom at a wedding, or a visitor on the road; to escort to one's home.

def: s.m. = angnā, q.v. s.m. Inclosed space adjoining a house; court-yard, court, area, quadrangle (also written angnā and angnāʼī: syn. ṣaḥn and ćauk).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀉𑀕𑁆𑀕𑁂𑀇 (uggei), from Sanskrit *उद्गाति (*udgāti). Cognate with Gujarati ઉગવું (ugvũ), Punjabi اُگّنا (uggnā) / ਉੱਗਣਾ (uggaṇā), Sindhi اُڳڻ (ug̠ṇ) / उॻणु (ug̠aṇu).

def: s.m. Anything appertaining to fire, or the deity of fire; the south-east quarter; — adj. Fiery, pungent, piquant, stimulating, digestive; — south-eastern.
s.f. = اگن agni, q.v.
more: n. f. Fire. agni bān , n. m. Ped. Burning arrows; a rocket; a fiery missile. agni bāo , n. f. The farcy in horses; an eruptive disease in men and elephants; erysipelas. agni-parikshā , Ordeal by fire. ag'ni-kon , n. f. The south-east quarter. agni-hotrī , n. m. 1. One who performs the ceremony of Hom; a fire-worshiper. 2. A sect of Brahmans. agni-hom , n. Performance of worship with fire; offering oblations to fire.
s. f. See اگن agni, Fire, &c.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अग्नि (agní). Doublet of آگ (āg) and اَگَن (agan).

def: s. m. Arrow of fire.
s.m. An insect of scarlet colour the lady-bird.
s.m. pl. (of غني g̠anī), The rich; independent people.
adj. Not obvious, imperceptible, invisible; unseen, unperceived; hidden, secret, mysterious; — s.m. The invisible Supreme Being.

agar ma͠i-ne ye kiyā hotā to

wety: Inherited from Sanskrit अगरु (agaru).

s.m. Confusior, jumble, medley, mess, hash; odds and ends; trifles, trumpery; trash, nonsense; — adj. Mixed, confused, jumbled; promiscuous, composed of odds and ends.
